title: Curated Articles
description: Hand-picked priority content about AI coding agents

links.title: Minions: Stripe's one-shot, end-to-end coding agents
links.author: Stripe Engineering
links.summary: Introducing Minions, Stripe's internal AI coding agents that complete tasks end-to-end in a single shot.

links.title: Minions: Stripe's one-shot, end-to-end coding agents (Part 2)
links.author: Stripe Engineering
links.summary: Deep dive into the architecture and implementation details of Stripe's Minions coding agents.

links.title: Stop Thinking of AI as a Coworker. It's an Exoskeleton.
links.author: Ben Gregory
links.summary: Organizations viewing AI as an autonomous agent tend to disappoint, while those treating it as an amplifier of human capability achieve better results. Advocates for a micro-agent architecture where discrete tasks receive AI support while humans retain decision-making authority.

links.title: AI Risk Management Framework
links.author: Prof. Hernan Huwyler, MBA CPA CAIO
links.summary: AI Risk Management Framework overview covering AI governance, compliance, and agentic AI considerations.

links.title: AI Coding Meme: This meme has been brilliantly updated
links.author: Andreas Horn
links.summary: Updated meme about AI coding capabilities and commentary on ADHD as a superpower in tech.

links.title: AI is going to break services firms long before it breaks the enterprise
links.author: David Suydam
links.summary: There's a competitive race where firms must restructure their delivery economics before clients force them to. AI should be treated as the operating system we live in, not a standalone product.

links.title: How I'm Productive with Claude Code
links.author: Neil Kakkar
links.summary: After months of experimentation, the author shares a workflow that maximizes Claude Code's effectiveness: treat it as a highly capable junior developer who needs clear requirements, frequent check-ins, and careful review.

links.title: Vibecoders Can't Build for Longevity
links.author: d11r
links.summary: The trend of 'vibe coding' - quickly prototyping with AI and shipping without deep understanding - creates software that works initially but becomes unmaintainable within months.

links.title: I tried to prove I'm not AI. My aunt wasn't convinced
links.author: BBC Future
links.summary: The author attempted various methods to prove human authenticity to an AI detector service and found the entire concept fundamentally broken.

links.title: So where are all the AI apps?
links.author: Answer.AI
links.summary: Despite massive investment in AI infrastructure, we're seeing remarkably few transformative consumer AI applications emerge. The gap between AI capability and useful products suggests fundamental challenges in productizing AI.

links.title: 90% of Claude-linked output going to GitHub repos with <2 stars
links.author: claudescode.dev
links.summary: Analysis of GitHub data suggests that the overwhelming majority of AI-generated code ends up in repositories with minimal community validation, raising questions about the quality and utility of AI coding assistance at scale.

links.title: Thoughts on slowing the fuck down
links.author: Mario Zechner
links.summary: AI coding agents encourage developers to move fast without understanding, producing mountains of AI-generated code that obscures rather than illuminates. True craftsmanship requires slowing down and maintaining ownership of every line.

links.title: Eight years of wanting, three months of building with AI
links.author: Lalit Maganti
links.summary: AI coding agents can unlock long-deferred projects by overcoming psychological barriers, but their effectiveness depends critically on task type—excelling at implementation while proving dangerous for architectural design decisions.

links.title: The threat is comfortable drift toward not understanding what you're doing
links.author: Ergosphere
links.summary: AI tools in academic research pose a subtle but profound danger - they enable students to bypass intellectual development and produce publishable work without building the foundational understanding necessary for independent scientific thinking.
